fix_plan.description: Miruro secure-pipe sources endpoint changed: streams[].url is now empty; the kwik.cx embed URL appears in the referer field. The dto.go sourcesResponse struct has an embeds[] field ('currently unused — present for forward-compat') — the real stream likely migrated there. Fix: (1) Fetch a live sources response to confirm new wire format (run from inside scraper container: docker exec animeenigma-scraper sh -c 'wget -qO- --header="Referer: https://www.miruro.tv/" "<secure-pipe-sources-url>"'). (2) If embeds[] now carries the kwik.cx URL (type='kwik'), add embed extraction via the existing kwik extractor in services/scraper/internal/embeds/. (3) If streams[] now has a different URL field name, update rawStream json tags. (4) Run go test -count=1 ./internal/providers/miruro/... — update goldens if format changed. (5) make redeploy-scraper and verify miruro.stream stage comes up. For nineanime: set enabled: false in docker/scraper-providers.yaml (it is the last-resort provider, probe anime now hits YouTube stubs — noise with no user benefit given allanime+animefever are healthy).
fix_plan.context: EN failover chain is allanime→animefever→miruro→nineanime. First two are healthy so users still get EN streams. But miruro and nineanime degradation means 0 fallback depth if allanime or animefever have a bad day. The miruro schema drift is likely from a recent upstream secure-pipe API update rotating the response shape (consistent with the 2026-06 comment in client.go about anilistId becoming required). Nineanime disable is low-risk: it was already the acknowledged last-resort sink per its doc.go.
fix_plan.verification: After fix: curl -s http://localhost:8088/metrics | grep 'provider_health_up{provider="miruro",stage="stream"}' should return 1. curl -s http://localhost:8000/api/anime/_/scraper/health should show miruro.stream up=true. For nineanime: verify it no longer appears in the health endpoint after being disabled.
